# mkdocx

Minimal markdown documentation builder. Static site generator built with Astro, Tailwind CSS, and Pagefind.

Version: 0.1.0
Install: pip install mkdocx  |  pnpm add mkdocx  |  npm install mkdocx


## What mkdocx is

mkdocx turns markdown files into a clean, fast documentation site. It provides:
- A monospace-first design system (Geist Mono)
- Full-text search (Pagefind)
- Syntax highlighting (Shiki, github-dark theme)
- Light and dark themes
- A 3-column layout: sidebar, content, table of contents
- Six built-in components: Callout, Tooltip, FileTree, Tabs, Tab, Image
- Native collapsible sections via HTML <details>


## Project structure

Minimum required files:

  your-project/
  ├── mkdocx.config.js       site configuration
  └── src/
      └── content/
          ├── docs/          documentation pages (.md or .mdx)
          └── writing/       dated blog posts (.md or .mdx, optional)

Files in docs/ become /docs/<slug> URLs.
Files in subdirectories (e.g., docs/guides/foo.md) become /docs/guides/foo.
Subdirectories create labeled section headers in the left sidebar.


## mkdocx.config.js

Full schema:

  export default {
    name: 'Your Project',            // required — site name, used in <title> and nav
    description: 'Short summary.',   // required — used in <meta name="description">
    nav: [                           // optional — top navigation links
      { label: 'docs', href: '/docs/getting-started' },
      { label: 'github', href: 'https://github.com/...' },
    ],
    footer: [                        // optional — footer links
      { label: 'License', href: '/license' },
    ],
    siteUrl: 'https://your-docs.com', // optional — for sitemaps and canonical URLs
    dateFormat: { year: 'numeric', month: 'short', day: 'numeric' }, // optional
    dateLocale: 'en-US',             // optional — BCP 47 locale
  }

All fields except name and description are optional.


## Frontmatter

Docs pages (src/content/docs/):

  ---
  title: Page title          # required
  description: Short text.   # optional, for <meta>
  order: 1                   # optional, sidebar sort position (lower = higher); default: 99
  ---

Writing posts (src/content/writing/):

  ---
  title: Post title          # required
  date: "2026-04-10"         # required, YYYY-MM-DD
  description: Short text.   # optional
  author: Name               # optional
  ---


## CLI commands

Node.js (pnpm / npm):

  pnpm dev              start dev server at http://localhost:4321
  pnpm build            production build to dist/ (Astro + Pagefind)
  pnpm preview          preview production build at http://localhost:3000

Python (pip install mkdocx):

  mkdocx dev [--port N]    start dev server
  mkdocx build             production build to dist/
  mkdocx preview           preview build locally
  mkdocx deploy <bucket>   build + sync dist/ to GCS bucket via gsutil

The Python CLI auto-detects the project root by walking up from CWD to find mkdocx.config.js.


## Build output

  dist/
  ├── index.html               homepage
  ├── docs/<slug>/index.html   one file per doc page
  ├── writing/<slug>/index.html
  ├── _astro/                  CSS + JS bundles (content-hashed)
  ├── sitemap.xml
  └── pagefind/                full-text search index


## Components

Components must be imported in .mdx files. They are NOT available in plain .md files (except Image, which replaces <img> automatically, and <details> which is native HTML).

Import syntax (at top of .mdx file):

  import Callout from '../../components/Callout.astro'
  import Tooltip from '../../components/Tooltip.astro'
  import FileTree from '../../components/FileTree.astro'
  import Tabs from '../../components/Tabs.astro'
  import Tab from '../../components/Tab.astro'

The path depth (../../) depends on the file's location relative to src/content/.


### Callout

Renders a bordered notice block. Three types: note (default), warning, tip.

  <Callout>This is a note.</Callout>
  <Callout type="warning">This action cannot be undone.</Callout>
  <Callout type="tip">Run dbq check before every deploy.</Callout>

Props:
  type: "note" | "warning" | "tip"   default: "note"


### Tooltip

Inline hover definition. Renders a dotted underline.

  The <Tooltip tip="Definition text here">term</Tooltip> appears inline.

Props:
  tip: string   required — the definition shown on hover


### FileTree

Collapsible file and directory tree. Folders open by default and collapse on click.

  <FileTree>
  - src/
    - content/
      - docs/
        - **getting-started.md**
    - components/
  - mkdocx.config.js
  </FileTree>

Syntax rules:
- Two spaces per indent level
- End directories with /
- Wrap filenames in **bold** to highlight them
- Links: add [href="/path"] after a filename (e.g., - index.astro [href="/"])

No props. Slot content is a markdown unordered list.


### Tabs

Tabbed panels for showing alternative instructions.

  <Tabs labels={["pnpm", "npm", "pip"]}>
  <Tab>
  ```bash
  pnpm add package
  ```
  </Tab>
  <Tab>
  ```bash
  npm install package
  ```
  </Tab>
  <Tab>
  ```bash
  pip install package
  ```
  </Tab>
  </Tabs>

Rules:
- labels array length must equal the number of <Tab> children
- <Tab> takes no props — slot wrapper only
- Use Tabs whenever the same action has multiple equivalent paths

Props (Tabs):
  labels: string[]   required


### Image

Drop-in replacement for markdown image syntax. Handles local and remote sources.

  ![Alt text](./local-image.png)            local, optimized at build time
  ![Alt text](/public-image.png)            served as-is from public/
  ![Alt text](./arch.png "Caption text")    with caption

Local images (from src/assets/) are resized and converted to WebP/AVIF at build time.
Public images (from public/) are served with lazy loading, no processing.

Props:
  src: string | ImageMetadata   required
  alt: string                   required
  width: number                 optional
  height: number                optional
  caption: string               optional


### Collapsible sections

Native HTML. Works in .md and .mdx files. No import needed.

  <details>
  <summary>Section title</summary>

  Content here. Markdown works: code blocks, tables, lists.

  </details>

Start expanded with the open attribute:

  <details open>
  <summary>Expanded by default</summary>
  ...
  </details>

Use for: long terminal output, optional reading, troubleshooting steps.


## Writing conventions

Style: precise, minimal, no filler.

- Short sentences. Each one carries weight.
- Remove "you can", "this allows you to", "in order to".
- Active voice. Present tense.
- Lead with what something is, not how to use it.
- One blank line between paragraphs in markdown.

Heading hierarchy:
  #    reserved — injected from frontmatter title, do not use in body
  ##   top-level sections
  ###  subsections
  #### avoid — restructure instead

Code blocks: always include a language tag (python, bash, yaml, sql, typescript, etc.).

Nested code fences: use four backticks for the outer fence when content contains triple backticks.

  ````markdown
  ```bash
  inner code block
  ```
  ````


## Page structure (recommended order)

1. What it is — one paragraph, no setup
2. Why it exists — skip if obvious from context
3. Basic usage — simplest working example
4. Full options / schema — table or annotated examples
5. Edge cases — only if non-obvious
6. Related links


## File organization

Subdirectories group pages under labeled sidebar sections.

  src/content/docs/
  ├── getting-started.md    → /docs/getting-started  (no section)
  ├── configuration.md      → /docs/configuration    (no section)
  └── guides/
      ├── custom-theme.md   → /docs/guides/custom-theme   (section: "guides")
      └── deployment.md     → /docs/guides/deployment     (section: "guides")

Sort order is controlled by the order frontmatter field. Use increments of 10.


## Deployment

The build output in dist/ is static HTML. Serve it anywhere.

Node.js:
  npx serve dist

Docker (nginx):
  FROM node:22-alpine AS builder
  WORKDIR /app
  COPY package*.json ./
  RUN npm ci
  COPY . .
  RUN npm run build

  FROM nginx:alpine
  COPY --from=builder /app/dist /usr/share/nginx/html
  EXPOSE 80

Python (GCS):
  mkdocx deploy gs://your-bucket

GCP Cloud Storage:
  pnpm build
  gsutil -m rsync -r -d dist/ gs://your-bucket/
  gsutil iam ch allUsers:objectViewer gs://your-bucket


## Common mistakes

- Using <Tabs> in a .md file (not .mdx) — components require .mdx
- Nested triple-backtick code fences — use four backticks for the outer fence
- Using # heading in body content — conflicts with the auto-injected title
- Forgetting to match labels array length with number of <Tab> children
- Putting src/assets/ images in public/ (no optimization) or public/ images in src/assets/ (build error)


## Performance

Static HTML, no server-side rendering.
  Lighthouse: 95+
  FCP: < 500ms
  Total JS: < 50KB (Pagefind loaded async, only on search open)
